title: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication
summary: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication uses an original volunteer profile incident scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html elements nesting, html attributes without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real volunteer profile incident artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

steps.title: Triage the volunteer profile incident · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which observation identifies the highest-value source boundary to test first? Work context: volunteer profile incident.
steps.why: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the volunteer profile incident.

content.title: volunteer profile incident · evidence decision
content.text: Commit before feedback, then connect the selected claim to the volunteer profile incident evidence.

Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication: compare the current artifact with the named predict goal for step 1.
Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “Triage the volunteer profile incident.”

options.text: Volunteer profile incident case 1: all elements are void elements, so closing tags are optional whenever the page still appears.
Volunteer profile incident case 1: parent-child relationships come from correctly nested tags; indentation should then mirror that structure.
Volunteer profile incident case 1: a browser screenshot proves the source tree is correctly nested.
